seoTitle: Track When People Open Your Emails With HubSpot
metaDescription: See how HubSpot tracks email opens using invisible pixels, plus how to install the Sales extension and review open timestamps in your CRM feed.
intro: HubSpot tracks whether someone opened your email using an invisible single-pixel image embedded in the message, which logs the moment it loads. This guide covers turning on that tracking, installing the Sales browser extension, and reading the resulting activity data. It ends with using real-time notifications to time your follow-up outreach.

steps.heading: Understand how tracking works
steps.body: Know that HubSpot uses an invisible tracking pixel to log when a recipient's email client loads your message.

steps.heading: Enable tracking in settings
steps.body: Go to your HubSpot CRM settings and activate default email and click tracking indicators.

steps.heading: Install the Sales extension
steps.body: Add the HubSpot Sales extension for Chrome, Outlook, or Safari to track emails sent from your regular inbox.

steps.heading: Verify tracking on a draft
steps.body: Draft a message and confirm the Track and Log checkboxes are enabled before sending.

steps.heading: Watch real-time notifications
steps.body: Keep an eye on desktop pop-up alerts that fire when a recipient opens your email.

steps.heading: Review contact-level history
steps.body: Open a contact's record timeline to see historical open events and exact timestamps.

faq.question: How does HubSpot know when someone opens my email?
faq.answer: It embeds an invisible single-pixel image in the message that logs an open event when the recipient's client loads it.

faq.question: Can I get a real-time alert when someone opens my email?
faq.answer: Yes, desktop pop-up notifications can alert you the moment a tracked email is opened.

faq.question: Does this work for emails sent from Gmail or Outlook too?
faq.answer: Yes, as long as the HubSpot Sales extension is installed and active in that inbox client.

faq.question: Can I see which links a recipient clicked?
faq.answer: Yes, the activity feed can be filtered to isolate contacts who clicked embedded links in your email.
